EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Genpact proposes a modern, AI‑first, agentic managed services platform designed to deliver significant structural cost reduction, outstanding service quality, and regulatory-ready scalability. The primary objective is to transition from traditional, human-led, transaction-based operations to human + AI agent-orchestrated operations across Customer Service, Risk Management, and Back Office functions.

Strategic Outcomes: Over the term of the agreement, our solution is expected to achieve a 68% uplift in productivity, with approximately 55% of this gain realized within the first three years. FIS stands to benefit from net savings of 53% over a seven-year horizon. In addition to financial improvements, the initiative will drive measurable enhancements in customer experience, resolution speed, fraud loss reduction, compliance adherence and average handle time (AHT). The solution will also establish a scalable foundation for expanding the TMS managed services portfolio.

Migration & Stabilization Approach: The migration strategy is structured around a three-wave rebadging and migration process covering North America, the UK, the Netherlands, and Germany. Operations will be stabilized before any decoupling occurs. Critical onshore activities will be retained where required by regulation or risk considerations, with a gradual geo-relocation to India and the Philippines. The approach is underpinned by proven rebadging playbooks that have demonstrated high acceptance and retention rates historically.

Transformation & Technology Strategy: The transformation approach includes a complete migration to NiCE CXone and modernization of the contact center technology stack. Existing FIS platforms, such as those within the Microsoft ecosystem, Pega, and automation tools, will be leveraged to maximize return on investment. Scalable agentic AI solutions will be deployed, including intelligent voice and chat agents, fraud and dispute analyst agents, and back-office service request and document intelligence agents.

Operating Model Evolution: The operating model will shift from linear queue-based processes to a pod-based, human-agent collaborative delivery approach. Genpact will introduce the Agentic Operations Readiness Acceleration (AORA) Framework, which focuses on operating model reinvention, role evolution towards exception-led supervision, continuous capability enhancement, and embedded Responsible AI governance.

Commercial Model & Risk Sharing: The commercial model is structured around a seven-year pro-forma with front-loaded value realization. Genpact demonstrates a willingness to co-invest upfront, recovering investments through gain-share mechanisms. Flexible financial re-engineering options are available to mitigate the Year-1 financial impact for FIS.

Genpact is a proven long-term transformation and value realization partner, integrating rebadging, technology modernization, agentic AI, and aligned commercial incentives that will future-proof FIS-TMS, all while ensuring rapid and measurable business outcomes.
